request.question: During the 2007 Oakland Athletics season, Ken Macha was replaced by a manager who now is the bench coach of what team?

title: 2017 Oakland Athletics season
2007 Oakland Athletics season
Don Wakamatsu
Bob Geren
Steve Staggs
1978 Oakland Athletics season
Rob Thomson
2014 Oakland Athletics season
Roger Smithberg
1980 Oakland Athletics season
sentences: The 2017 Oakland Athletics season was the 50th for the Oakland Athletics franchise in Oakland (all at Oakland Coliseum), as well as the 117th in club history.
The A's began the season on April 3 at home against Los Angeles Angels of Anaheim.
They will finish the season at the Texas Rangers on October 1.
The Oakland Athletics' 2007 season was their first since 2004 in which they defended a title.
The A's were without Frank Thomas (Toronto) and ace Barry Zito (San Francisco), both players who the A's lost to free agency.
They filled the space left by Frank Thomas by signing free agent catcher Mike Piazza to a one-year contract worth $8.5 million to be the team's designated hitter.

They were also without Ken Macha, their former manager who was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren, as well as third-base coach Ron Washington, who accepted the managerial position of the Texas Rangers.
It was their first losing season since 1998.
Wilbur Donald Wakamatsu (born February 22, 1963) is an American former professional baseball player, scout, manager and current coach.
He played as a catcher in Major League Baseball for the Chicago White Sox.
He was hired as bench coach of the Kansas City Royals for the 2014 season.
He was the manager of the Seattle Mariners for the season, as well as the majority of the season.
He was the Toronto Blue Jays' bench coach for 2011 and 2012, after which he was replaced by DeMarlo Hale.
During the 2013 season he worked as a scout for the New York Yankees in the Dallas/Fort Worth area.
Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.
During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).
He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.
He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.
Stephen Robert Staggs (born May 6, 1951) is a former Major League Baseball second baseman who played for two seasons.
He played 72 games for the Toronto Blue Jays during the 1977 Toronto Blue Jays season and 47 games for the Oakland Athletics during the 1978 Oakland Athletics season.
The 1978 Oakland Athletics season was the team's eleventh in Oakland, California.
The team sought to rebound from its first losing season in a decade (a 63-99 result in 1977).
Despite low expectations, the Athletics remained competitive for nearly three-quarters of the season.
Despite posting a respectable 61-56 mark through 117 games, the Athletics collapsed in the season's final weeks; their 8-37 finish ensured a second consecutive season of fewer than 70 wins.
Robert Lewis Thomson (born August 16, 1963) is a Canadian bench coach for the New York Yankees.
He previously served as bench coach during the 2008 season, third base coach from 2009 - 2014, and returned to serving as bench coach in 2015.
He has also served as Major League Field Coordinator for the organization.
The 2014 Oakland Athletics season was the 46th for the franchise at O.co Coliseum, as well as the 114th in club history.
The Athletics entered the season hoping to win a third consecutive American League West championship; to that end, the team made a number of key signings and trades during the 2013-14 MLB offseason.
Notably, Athletics traded outfielder Michael Choice for left fielder Craig Gentry and pitcher Josh Lindblom; they also traded the promising but oft-injured Brett Anderson for reliever Drew Pomeranz.
Additional trades brought in relievers Fernando Abad (acquired for John Wooten), Luke Gregerson (acquired for Seth Smith), and Jim Johnson (acquired for Jemile Weeks and David Freitas).
In free agency, the Athletics signed former All-Star starting pitcher Scott Kazmir to a two-year deal.
These moves, among others, sought to bolster the depth of team's starting pitching and bullpen.
Roger Craig Smithberg (born March 21, 1966) is a former Major League Baseball pitcher who played for two seasons.
He pitched for the Oakland Athletics for 13 games during the 1993 Oakland Athletics season and two games during the 1994 Oakland Athletics season.
The 1980 Oakland Athletics season was the team's thirteenth season in Oakland.
The A's, under first-year manager Billy Martin, began the season with low expectations following their insipid 1979 campaign.

Strong performances from pitchers Mike Norris, Matt Keough, and Rick Langford, along with the brilliant play of breakout star (and future Hall-of-Famer) Rickey Henderson, paved the way for a staggering 29-win increase over the previous year's output.
The Athletics, only one year removed from baseball's worst record, swung to a second-place finish behind their 83-79 record.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: During the 2007 Oakland Athletics season, Ken Macha was replaced by a manager who now is the bench coach of what team?
hop1_docs: 2007 Oakland Athletics season | The Oakland Athletics' 2007 season was their first since 2004 in which they defended a title.  The A's were without Frank Thomas (Toronto) and ace Barry Zito (San Francisco), both players who the A's lost to free agency.  They filled the space left by Frank Thomas by signing free agent catcher Mike Piazza to a one-year contract worth $8.5 million to be the team's designated hitter.  They were also without Ken Macha, their former manager who was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren, as well as third-base coach Ron Washington, who accepted the managerial position of the Texas Rangers.  It was their first losing season since 1998.

Ken Macha | Kenneth Edward Macha ( ; born September 29, 1950, in Monroeville, Pennsylvania) is a former Major League Baseball third baseman and manager.  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2003–2006, including American League Western Division championships in both his first and final seasons with the team, and later managed the Milwaukee Brewers (2009–10).

Bob Geren | Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.  During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.  He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.

Vern Hoscheit | Vernard Arthur Hoscheit (April 1, 1922 – June 11, 2007) was an American professional baseball catcher, coach and manager.  He served as a coach on four World Series championship Major League Baseball teams with the Oakland Athletics and the New York Mets.  Hoscheit was the Mets' bullpen coach from 1984 to 1987, which included their World Series run in 1986.  During that season's spring training, Hoscheit accurately predicted that the team would clinch the National League East Division title on September 17, long before any regular season game was even played.

2010 Arizona Diamondbacks season | The Arizona Diamondbacks' 2010 season, the franchise's 13th season in Major League Baseball, included the team's attempt to make the playoffs for the first time since 2007.  On July 1, 2010, the Arizona Diamondbacks fired Manager A. J. Hinch following a 31-48 start to the 2010 season and promoted Kirk Gibson who had served as the bench coach of the team since the 2007 season.  Along with the dismissal of Hinch came the firing of General Manager Josh Byrnes.

List of Oakland Athletics managers | The Oakland Athletics are a professional baseball team based in Oakland, California.  Before moving to Oakland in 1968, the team played in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania from 1901 through 1954 and in Kansas City, Missouri from 1955 through 1967.  The Athletics are members of the American League (AL) West division in Major League Baseball (MLB).  In baseball, the head coach of a team is called the manager, or more formally, the field manager.  The duties of the team manager include team strategy and leadership on and off the field.  The team has employed 30 different managers in its history.  The current Athletics' manager is Bob Melvin.

Don Wakamatsu | Wilbur Donald Wakamatsu (born February 22, 1963) is an American former professional baseball player, scout, manager and current coach.  He played as a catcher in Major League Baseball for the Chicago White Sox.  He was hired as bench coach of the Kansas City Royals for the 2014 season.  He was the manager of the Seattle Mariners for the season, as well as the majority of the season.  He was the Toronto Blue Jays' bench coach for 2011 and 2012, after which he was replaced by DeMarlo Hale.  During the 2013 season he worked as a scout for the New York Yankees in the Dallas/Fort Worth area.
response.summary_1: Passage [1] states Ken Macha "was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren." Bridge entity: Bob Geren. Missing fact: which team Bob Geren is currently the bench coach of. Next-hop retrieval: look up "Bob Geren current bench coach" or his biography/coach staff listing (anchor text to use: "replaced by Bob Geren").
response.hop2_query: Bob Geren bench coach current team
hop2_docs: Bob Geren | Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.  During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.  He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.

Bob Schaefer | Robert Walden Schaefer (born May 22, 1944, in Putnam, Connecticut) is a former interim manager, bench coach and farm system official in American Major League Baseball.  He is currently the special assistant to the general manager with the Washington Nationals.

2007 Oakland Athletics season | The Oakland Athletics' 2007 season was their first since 2004 in which they defended a title.  The A's were without Frank Thomas (Toronto) and ace Barry Zito (San Francisco), both players who the A's lost to free agency.  They filled the space left by Frank Thomas by signing free agent catcher Mike Piazza to a one-year contract worth $8.5 million to be the team's designated hitter.  They were also without Ken Macha, their former manager who was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren, as well as third-base coach Ron Washington, who accepted the managerial position of the Texas Rangers.  It was their first losing season since 1998.

Bob Hallberg | Bob Hallberg (born February 10, 1944) is a college basketball coach and is currently the head coach of the women's team at St. Xavier University.  Prior to going to St. Xavier University, he was head coach at University of Illinois at Chicago and Chicago State University.

Bob Mumma | Bob Mumma (born March 16, 1971) is an American college baseball coach, currently serving as head coach of the UMBC Retrievers baseball team.  He was named to that position at the start of the 2012 season.

Pete Geren | Preston Murdoch Geren, III, known as Pete Geren (born January 29, 1952) served as the 20th United States Secretary of the Army from July 16, 2007 to September 16, 2009.  He is a Democratic former member of the United States House of Representatives from Texas's 12th congressional district.  He is currently president of the Sid W. Richardson Foundation in Fort Worth.

Bob Bicknell | Bob Bicknell (born November 13, 1969) is an American football coach and former player.  He is currently the wide receivers coach for the Baylor Bears football team of the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA), a position he has held since March 2017.  Bicknell is the son of former Boston College head coach Jack Bicknell and the younger brother of Jack Bicknell, Jr., the current assistant offensive line coach for the Miami Dolphins.
response.summary_2: Ken Macha was replaced by Bob Geren, who is currently the bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.
